pattern: The minimal powers of two summing to n are exactly n's set bits; a prefix sum of their exponents turns each product query into modular exponentiation.
hints: The unique minimal set of powers of two summing to n is just the binary representation of n, taken from the lowest bit up.
Multiplying a contiguous run of these powers means adding their exponents, so track a prefix sum of exponents.
For a query [l, r], the answer is 2 raised to (prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]) taken modulo 1e9+7 via fast exponentiation.

edge_inputs.description: n is one so the only power is two-to-the-zero
power of two n has a single element
full range product of all bits
large n with many set bits

stress.description: random n and many valid in-range queries against its bit count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    bits = bin(n).count('1')
    q = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randint(0, bits - 1)
        r = rng.randint(l, bits - 1)
        queries.append([l, r])
    return [n, queries]
stress.note: indices clamped to the number of set bits so every query is valid
